## PAGE 00790: Managing Excel and CSV file

- page_id: `136710435`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710435/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:38.661+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

425937017

425937023

425937014

**On this page**

****

425937022

##### Using UML properties upon importUsing UML properties upon import

If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.

To use UML properties upon import

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import

By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.

- If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).

To skip empty rows/columns via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the numberEmptyRowsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.
5. Select the numberEmptyColumnsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
6. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.
7. Click **Close**.

To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the **JAVA_ARGS** line, add the following values, and replace *POSITIVE_NUMBER* with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### To export by IDMapping elements by ID upon export

By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.

If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.

To map elements by ID via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **exportByIdentificationProperty** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To map elements by ID via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Color marking modified data upon export

If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.

- If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- To apply color marking, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Excel and CSV file' space='MD2024x'] must be enabled first.
- Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon Read From File action.

To color mark modified data via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **markExportedRows** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To color mark modified data via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:

- Red - removed
- Blue - updated
- Green - added

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table

To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Open File . The linked file is opened.

##### Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table

If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Synchronize with default mapping options' space='MD2024x']>]]>

To remove it, follow the procedure below.

To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Clear Selection . The reference between tables is removed.

import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b2d6e3a-8c64-4300-99b1-5479c4f00fe4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li>The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To skip empty rows/columns via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>numberEmptyRowsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.</span></li><li>Select the <strong>numberEmptyColumnsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource»  stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li><p>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, add the following values, and replace <span><em>POSITIVE_NUMBER</em> with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8d1721b-4d65-424e-b438-1d455094bac5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dnumber.empty.rows.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER
````html
-Dnumber.empty.columns.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ac8d2e-7190-4ed9-bbc8-ab71db7240b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">To export by ID</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Mapping elements by ID upon export</h3><p>By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="998aa366-976e-4d34-8afa-5cf0b8c1d789"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To map elements by ID via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <span><strong>exportByIdentificationProperty</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button. </span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To map elements by ID via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Color marking modified data upon export</h3><p>If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a294723e-2a8e-428f-a27b-dd6198b28341"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li>To apply color marking, <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="To export by ID"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ mapping elements by ID ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ac:link></strong>must be enabled first.</li><li>Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon <strong>Read From File</strong> action. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To color mark modified data via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <span><strong>markExportedRows</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To color mark modified data via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br />As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:</p><ul><li>Red - removed</li><li>Blue - updated</li><li>Green - added</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking_exported_data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><span style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table</span></h3><p>To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open File</strong>.<br />The linked file is opened.</li></ol><h3>Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Synchronize with default mapping options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read step #3 to link Excel or CSV file with the table >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To remove it, follow the procedure below. </span></p><p>To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Clear Selection</strong>.<br />The reference between tables is removed.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715262 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00791: Managing hyperlinks

- page_id: `136715262`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715262/Managing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:46:37.721+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing.

It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.

See the following pages for more information:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing. </p><p>It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.</p><p>See the following pages for more information:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="283fbdb0-2c56-4fb9-95d4-a701e4f8228e" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711480 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00792: Managing Map Groups

- page_id: `136711480`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711480/Managing+Map+Groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.034+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2091736993

2091736997

2091736996

**On this page**

33

2091736992

##### Introduction

The purpose of the Map Groups is to simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024x']>]]>

It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.

The following figure demonstrates the *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group that contains **CarBreak Requirments**, **C*arBreak Structure*** and *Car Break Satisfy*Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

##### Creating Map Groups

To create a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** .
2. In the **Tree** tab of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog, select the *Excel/CSV imports*Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Specification window opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Name** property value, type the name for the new Map Group and click the **Close** button. 
The map group is created and becomes visible in the **Select Map Group for Editing**drop-down list box of the**Manage Maps Groups**dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.

##### Deleting Map Groups

To delete the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** .
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. Click the **Delete**button. The Map Group is deleted.

##### Adding import maps to a Map Group

To add Import Maps to a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']to add only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add it recursively. The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group

##### Removing import maps from a Map Group

To remove an Import Map from a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to remove.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove them recursively. The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.

##### Reordering import maps in the Map Group

By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.

To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to move the selected Import Map up or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to move the Import Map down. The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.

2091741873

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f688b16-cc9f-482c-8ef7-e0111f1b899f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091736993</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="297d72a4-1f55-4194-9bfe-996c65a81826"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091736997</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10ee678-ddf2-4b0e-ae41-f93d8a2cc114"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091736996</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0ffa1c-34c3-40ac-b751-1b0d101db32a"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a784983-ee6f-40db-b178-0780373962f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091736992</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>The purpose of the Map Groups is to <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually</span>. You can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6525ad99-63f6-4e7e-b60f-e0acc53df5e6">collect saved Import</ac:inline-comment-marker> Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="38f3ca03-e390-4671-9d01-dc3cbe3e94fc">and then</ac:inline-comment-marker> add sav<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8b4b3294-b3c1-4dce-a83a-645a5d5a2cfa">ed Import Maps.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save Import Map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0371a7b-7feb-4cf2-b478-dcb3347cfefc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure demonstrates the <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group that contains <em><em>CarBreak Requirments</em></em>, <em><em>C<em>arBreak Structure</em></em></em> and <em>Car Break Satisfy </em>Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_map_group_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</h6><h3>Creating Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog, select the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>Excel/CSV imports</em> </span>Package as the owner for th<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7ec4d44-e19f-41c8-bd90-7578977d0e96">e new Map Group.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. T<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he Specification window opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_group_specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Name</strong> property value, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">type the name for the new <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Map Group</span> and click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The map group is created and becomes visible in the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing </strong>drop-down list box <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">of the </span><strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog</span>. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Map Group</span>.</span></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Deleting Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To delete the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Map Group</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option and select the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Map Group</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button. <br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Map Group</span> is deleted.</span></li></ol><h3>Adding import maps to a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To add Import Maps to a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. T<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he </span><strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_recursively_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add it recursively.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group</span></li></ol><h3>Removing import maps from a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To remove an Import Map from a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. T<span>he </span><strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong><span> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to remove.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_all_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove them recursively.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.</span></li></ol><h3>Reordering import maps in the Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.</p><p>To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="up_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to move the selected Import Map up or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="down_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to move the Import Map down.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a25a9b16-709e-4690-8242-a478bd5195c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091741873</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files">Importing data from Excel or CSV files</a></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Saving+Import+Map">Saving Import Map</a></p></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715260 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00793: Managing navigation in model

- page_id: `136715260`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715260/Managing+navigation+in+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:17.278+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements.

See these pages for more details:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing double-click behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing hyperlinks' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements. </p><p>See these pages for more details:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing double-click behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing hyperlinks" /></ac:link> </li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713263 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00794: Managing project usages

- page_id: `136713263`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713263/Managing+project+usages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:44:42.691+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

650067215

650067218

650067217

To manage project usages, you must have **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024x'].

Large Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects are built from smaller parts.

We recommend project partitioning when the model has weakly dependent parts, such as type libraries, models of different phases of software/system development, etc. A part of the model moved to a separate project can be used in many projects. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a*project usage* or *used project*.

You can easily detach a selected part of the model by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting a package to a new server project' space='MD2024x'] it to a separate TWCloud project and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024x'] it in other server projects. Since projects are used as *read-only*, you cannot modify their contents directly from the main project. To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing the content of a used project' space='MD2024x']a used project, you should open it as stand-alone. If there is a need, you can simply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024x'] to another used project version.

If you no longer need a fragment of the model in a separate project, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing the content of a used project into your project' space='MD2024x']the project into the main project. Additionally, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using the project in your project' space='MD2024x']using the project.

You can manage TWCloud project usages in the **Project Usages** dialog, which opens after you select **Options** > **Project Usages**.

650067214

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f58cef8f-b350-4268-925d-ac5352329791"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd332d6b-dad5-41a7-a0b8-bd0a4a7023bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067218</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d1d34f7-5f00-4396-ab37-3520b0401d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067217</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To manage project usages, you must have <strong>Administer Resources</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[permission]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Large Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects are built from smaller parts.</p><p>We recommend project partitioning when the model has weakly dependent parts, such as type libraries, models of different phases of software/system development, etc. A part of the model moved to a separate project can be used in many projects. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a<em> project usage</em> or <em>used project</em>.</p><p>You can easily detach a selected part of the model by <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Exporting a package to a new server project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exporting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it to a separate TWCloud project and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[re-use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it in other server projects. Since projects are used as <em>read-only</em>, you cannot modify their contents directly from the main project. To <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Editing the content of a used project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modify ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>a used project, you should open it as stand-alone. If there is a need, you can simply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to another used project version.</p><p>If you no longer need a fragment of the model in a separate project, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing the content of a used project into your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the project into the main project. Additionally, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stop ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>using the project.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4c85e912-3baa-41a5-b10c-1df34e108d3e">You can manage TWCloud project usages</ac:inline-comment-marker> in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project Usages</strong> dialog, which opens after you select <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Options</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project Usages</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff8637e6-107e-4535-b951-4a13817d9662"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067214</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b166baea-bc94-4e3a-8dde-7e44d26c5046" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711340 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00796: Managing regions

- page_id: `136711340`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711340/Managing+regions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:33.915+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['create-region', 'managing-regions', 'remove-region', 'insert-state', 'hide-region']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#createregionTo create a region

Do one of the following:

- In the Containment tree, right-click the State, select Create Element , and then click Region .
- On the diagram, right-click the State, then select the Add New Region .
- On the diagram, select the State symbol and click the **Create Region**button [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav3124c17bcd90e77e6ec5a12cec700032.png'] .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group, then click Create and select Region . Specify the region and click Close or Back .

To remove a region

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram, right-click the State and select Remove Region .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group. Then click the region and click the Delete button.

To insert a State to the region quickly

- In the Containment tree or on the diagram, select the State, and drag it to the State's region.

To display or hide the region name

1. On the diagram, right-click the State and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, in the right-top corner, click to expand the menu, and select the Expert mode.
3. Select or clear the Show Region Name check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713020 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00797: Managing server projects

- page_id: `136713020`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713020/Managing+server+projects

- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:13.919+02:00`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage your server projects, as well as locally saved server projects (known as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='MD2024x']), in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

Currently open project is automatically selected in the**Manage Projects**dialog, making it easier to findit in the project list.

To open the **Manage Projects**dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting a collaboration session' space='MD2024x']
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Projects .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use the search tab to quickly filter used projects by name.

In the **Manage Projects** dialog, you can:

- For server projects:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x']
  - Rename a server project
  - Remove a server project
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cloning projects' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing historical versions of the project' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Branching projects' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Password-protected projects' space='MD2024x']
- For offline server projects:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713342 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00798: Managing Teamwork Cloud users

- page_id: `136713342`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713342/Managing+Teamwork+Cloud+users
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:28.322+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Managing Teamwork Cloud users includes:

- Creating and modifying user accounts
- Creating roles and assigning them to the users
- Assigning the users to projects

These tasks can be performed only in Teamwork Cloud Administrative Console. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application' space='TWCloud2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources application' space='TWCloud2024x'] in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Managing Teamwork Cloud users includes:</p><ul><li>Creating and modifying user accounts</li><li>Creating roles and assigning them to the users</li><li>Assigning the users to projects</li></ul><p> </p><p>These tasks can be performed only in Teamwork Cloud Administrative Console. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Users application" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Resources application" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Roles Application]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709627 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00799: Managing unresolved references

- page_id: `136709627`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709627/Managing+unresolved+references
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-08T14:58:42.542+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1525838642

INLINE

1525838654

INLINE

1526101509

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1525838644

INLINE

An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved.

##### Running composition integrity analysis

If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A summary of validation errors in the status line.

To run the composition integrity analysis

1. Do either: The **Validation Results** panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis In the **Active Validation Results** panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select **Run Composition Integrity Analysis**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis in Model BrowserIn the Model Browser, right-click an el ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select Validation > Recovered Element > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png'] > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png']

##### Identifying validation errors

These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:

- REF : The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project.
- SRE : T he symbol usage of the recovered element. Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error.
- URE : The element referencing the recovered element.

##### Handling recovered elements

It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:

- Eliminate the causes of the element recovery . This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.
- Restore recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.
- Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.

The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *[ATTACHMENT filename='Project Decomposition Description.mdzip']*****sample model that uses the following projects: *[ATTACHMENT filename='Analysis and Design.mdzip'],**[ATTACHMENT filename='Code Level.mdzip'],* and***[ATTACHMENT filename='domain.mdzip'] .*

Alternatively, you can open the model from*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\project decomposition .*
