<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709617 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00800: Managing used projects

- page_id: `136709617`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709617/Managing+used+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:32:32.999+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: ['used-projects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305472217

305472219

305472218

You can manage usages of an open local project in the**Used Projects**dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024x'].

To specify project usage options

1. Do one of the following: • From the Options menu, select Project Usages . • Right-click a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Options . The Used Projects dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='used_projects_dialog.png']
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Specify the following project usage options: Dialog element nameDescription**Accessibility****Read-only**- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.**Read-write**- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.**Load Mode****Always load** (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.**Autoload** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. MagicDraw monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.**Autoload with prompt** - this mode is similar to the **Autoload** mode, except that MagicDraw asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.**Manual load** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified. 
Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.**Use Index**Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.**Packages**this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.
  - Use the following commands: CommandDescription**Use Project**Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024x']** wizard opens.**Remove** > **Remove with References**Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.**Remove** > **Remove, keep references**Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:**Case #1**: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space='MD2024x'].**Case #2**: You may need to refactor projects that are created with MagicDraw versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of MagicDraw have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.**Import**Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:Is not a MagicDraw native projectIs locked (valid for server projects only)Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner 
structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.**Reload**Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.**Unload**Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.**Open Project**Click to open the selected used project for editing.**Options**Click to open the **Project Options** dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.
3. Click**OK**.

305472216

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4959a0d0-1838-422e-b107-470f07310bdf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305472217</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4caa6dc-8307-41ca-a615-bcbc56585653"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305472219</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d5aed4b-bfab-43a5-9f2c-2bb3f591d379"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305472218</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can manage usages of an open local project in the </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></p><p>To specify project usage options</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br />• From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong>.<br />• Right-click a used project in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3f8fd666-ee51-49d8-bfb1-b04a3433325b"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </ac:inline-comment-marker> and, from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog opens.<br /> <br /> <ac:image ac:title="Used Projects dialog" ac:alt="Used Projects dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_projects_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bf61027b-a39a-45ad-8754-b4d516a9a13c">Do one or both of the following:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li><p>Specify the following project usage options:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Dialog element name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Accessibility</strong></td><td><ul><li><strong>Read-only </strong>- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.</li><li><strong>Read-write </strong>- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Load Mode</strong></td><td><ul><li><p><strong>Always load</strong> (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. MagicDraw monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload with prompt</strong> - this mode is similar to the <strong>Autoload</strong> mode, except that MagicDraw asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Manual load</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified.<br />Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Use Index</strong></td><td>Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Packages</strong></td><td>this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.</td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span></p><ul><li><p>Use the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Use Project</strong></td><td><p>Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong> wizard opens.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove with References</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove, keep references</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:</p><p><strong>Case #1</strong>: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.</p><p>In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[replacing the initially used project with another project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Case #2</strong>: You may need to refactor projects that are created with MagicDraw versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of MagicDraw have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Import</strong></td><td><p>Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:</p><ul><li>Is not a MagicDraw native project</li><li>Is locked (valid for server projects only)</li><li>Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)</li></ul><p>The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner<br />structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.</p><p>Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Reload</strong></td><td><p>Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Unload</strong></td><td><p>Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.</p><p>This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Open Project</strong></td><td>Click to open the selected used project for editing.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul></li><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Click </span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">OK</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5ef2388-16e9-4815-b421-42a5cc71ce49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305472216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="16d34413-f793-43f6-98d3-4ff8a8242f9c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712808 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00801: Mandatory Tagged Value

- page_id: `136712808`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712808/Mandatory+Tagged+Value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:55.338+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

LackOfTagVal

**Description**

This validation rule checks that if the stereotype's tag definition multiplicity is 1, only one value to a slot is allowed.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Element

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the tag has exactly 1 value.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Car*stereotype contains a tag definition *gearTransmission*with the multiplicity set to one. The class *Sedan*with the applied stereotype *Car*does not have the *gearTransmission*tag specified with a value, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by specifying the *gearTransmission*tag with a value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>LackOfTagVal</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks that if the stereotype's <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e14238d-efa6-4400-84e7-8fcaa3e3a05b">tag definition</ac:inline-comment-marker> multiplicity is 1, only one value to a slot is allowed.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Element</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the tag has exactly 1 value.<strong> </strong></li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="96d08a8d-61b6-4fb5-9ed4-dd630b18b829">Example</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mandatory Tagged Value.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Mandatory Tagged Value" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>Car </em>stereotype contains a tag definition <em>gearTransmission </em>with the multiplicity set to one. The class <em>Sedan </em>with the applied stereotype <em>Car </em>does not have the <em>gearTransmission </em>tag specified with a value, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by specifying the <em>gearTransmission </em>tag with a value.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711122 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00802: Manifestations

- page_id: `136711122`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711122/Manifestations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:25.201+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1058526979

1058526981

1058526980

An artifact embodies or manifests a number of model elements. It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.

To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.

To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape

- On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select Artifacts .
- In the component Symbol Properties dialog, cancel the selection of the Suppress Artifacts check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1058527124

**Related pages**

- Model Elements
- Component diagram
- Specification Window
- Artifact
- Compartment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72de48ea-04ff-4c42-b49f-d48c2a190821"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526979</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4af85558-0fa6-4cfc-962c-c73d4205308e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526981</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="525018e6-c9fe-4c12-aa94-748358241404"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526980</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An artifact embodies or manifests a number of model elements. It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.</p><p>To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select <strong>Artifacts</strong>.</li><li>In the component <strong>Symbol Properties </strong>dialog, cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress Artifacts</strong> check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manifestation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manifestations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a790420-28ce-4670-a9b5-66ce00246c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058527124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Component+diagram">Component diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window">Specification Window</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CBMP2024x/Artifacts">Artifact</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Compartments">Compartment</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709971 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00803: Manipulations in the relation map

- page_id: `136709971`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709971/Manipulations+in+the+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:18.895+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-suppress-expand', 'relation-map-zoom', 'relation-map-hide', 'relation-map-remove', 'relation-map-restore-refresh', 'relation-map-validate', 'relation-map-decompose', 'relation-map-create-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manipulate and analyze your relation map in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] branches
- Move [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] an element
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x']

suppressTo suppress branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_button.png'] near the Node.

expandTo expand branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] near the Node.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

move_structureTo move the relation map structure

- Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it. The whole structure will be moved appropriately.

move_nodeTo move the selected Node

- Click a Node and drag it. The selected Node will be moved appropriately.

zoom_inTo zoom in the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.
- Click the Zoom In button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad +.

zoom_outTo zoom out the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.
- Click the Zoom Out button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad -.

fitTo fit in window

Do one of the following:

- Click the Fit in Window button [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_in_window.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+W.

zoom_1_1To zoom 1:1

- Click the Zoom 1:1 button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_1_1.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad /.

hideTo hide an existing element in the diagram

- Press Delete.

removeTo remove an existing element from the model

- Press Ctrl+D.

restoreTo restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the View toolbar. If the**Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements** option in the **Environment Options** dialog > **Diagrams** > **Relation Map**, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.

invalidTo start the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024x']of the relation map

- Click the Validate Diagram button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.

containment_treeTo select an element in the Containment tree

- Select the element and click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_in_containment_tree.png'] on the Navigation toolbar.
- On the selected element shortcut menu, click Select in Containment Tree .

decomposeTo decompose an element

1. Select an element.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected element shortcut menu, select Create Related Element .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_element_button.png'] or press Insert (or [ATTACHMENT filename='command-button.jpg'] + I).
3. Select element type and relationship. If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manipulations in the relation map' space='MD2024x'] 
The element is decomposed.

openTo open an existing element list for the unnamed element

1. Select unnamed element.
2. Do one of the following: - Click the left mouse button. - Press Space. An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.

same_typeTo create element of the same type

1. Type element name.
2. Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can manipulate and analyze your relation map in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="suppress"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Suppress]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="expand"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expand ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>branches</li><li>Move<ac:link ac:anchor="move_structure"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ the whole structure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="move_node"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the selected Node]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="zoom_in"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Zoom in]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="zoom_out"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[zoom out]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="fit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Fit in Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="zoom_1_1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Zoom 1:1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link> an element</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="restore"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restore the layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="invalid"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See invalid elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="containment_tree"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select an element in the Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="decompose"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Decompose an element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="open"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opening an existing element list for the unnamed element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="same_type"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create element of the same type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link></li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b380cad-ea8b-4b8d-9354-5ef36e339e5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">suppress</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To suppress branches</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Suppress branches" ac:alt="Suppress branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="suppress_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> near the Node.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7d76cd0-3aab-4880-ae5e-69f288179fa7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">expand</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To expand branches</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Expand branches" ac:alt="Expand branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> near the Node.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Expand/suppress branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_expand_suppress.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76c6a8f1-da26-4e09-8bc5-e5b4ba9da7a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move_structure</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move the relation map structure</p><hr /><ul><li>Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it.<br />The whole structure will be moved appropriately.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ebe64415-e784-4134-8714-1af281161f81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move_node</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move the selected Node</p><hr /><ul><li>Click a Node and drag it.<br />The selected Node will be moved appropriately.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="001edc97-9d56-4a7f-b5a8-441b766369ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_in</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom in the relation map structure</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.</li><li>Click the Zoom In button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom In" ac:alt="Zoom In"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_in.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad +.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0228966-ba90-46c4-824d-7786aabedc04"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_out</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom out the relation map structure</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.</li><li>Click the Zoom Out button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom Out" ac:alt="Zoom Out"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_out.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad -.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e532f24-ebfe-4a76-9ca2-3588a3623f58"><ac:parameter ac:name="">fit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To fit in window</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click the Fit in Window button <ac:image ac:title=" Fit in Window" ac:alt=" Fit in Window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_in_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+W.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78bd009d-cbc8-4663-a821-38f293944c11"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_1_1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom 1:1</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Zoom 1:1 button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom 1:1"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_1_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad /.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7c7644f-2853-4598-93c5-c1672beb8f69"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To hide an existing element in the diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Delete.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70c90757-28da-4b8d-ba76-7799e2b5e01c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove an existing element from the model</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Ctrl+D.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d51d368-a785-477d-8df2-d2eb0a669529"><ac:parameter ac:name="">restore</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the View toolbar.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="001a79c8-a148-42b4-88d1-8361f28777b5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the<strong> Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements</strong> option in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog &gt; <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Relation Map</strong>, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41d38c8a-ccdb-4a9c-a4ce-e771f051e101"><ac:parameter ac:name="">invalid</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To start the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Validate Diagram button <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db975f0e-96b7-4a2f-bc7c-194149d5cec7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">containment_tree</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select an element in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the element and click <ac:image ac:title="Select an element in the Containment tree" ac:alt="Select an element in the Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the Navigation toolbar.</li><li>On the selected element shortcut menu, click<strong> Select in Containment Tree</strong>.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fba24b3-7c73-4698-a562-231e40bd460e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">decompose</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To decompose an element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected element shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Related Element</strong>.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Related Element" ac:alt="Create Related Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or press Insert (or <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="command-button.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>+ I).</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select element type and relationship.  If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. <ac:link ac:anchor="open"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open an existing element list for the unnamed last element >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manipulations in the relation map" /></ac:link><br />The element is decomposed.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20193be0-2b01-422f-9587-a0ecd1639aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">open</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open an existing element list for the unnamed element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select unnamed element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Click the left mouse button.<br /> - Press Space.<br />An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6481eb5-ed02-4c78-a43f-0bef60502651"><ac:parameter ac:name="">same_type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create element of the same type</p><hr /><ol><li>Type element name.</li><li>Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711785 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00804: Manual numbering

- page_id: `136711785`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711785/Manual+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:55.586+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism > Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['how-to-update-the-last-id']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Using the manual numbering, you can:

- Set the ID for one or more elements.
- Increase, decrease, or change a number for an element.
- Add a prefix for the element number.
- Change a numbering format.
- Update the last ID.

To number the element

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the element list on the right side of the dialog, select an element you want to number.
3. Click Create .

To change the element’s numbering symbol

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click the Edit button.
  - If you need to change just the last number of the element ID, click **Edit**. The **Edit ID** dialog opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can enter only the number, if the selected element’s numbering value is numerical. 
You can enter only a new literal symbol, if the selected element’s numbering value is a literal symbol.
  - If you need to change the whole element ID (including the prefix), click Set Custom ID . The Custom ID dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Custom_ID_dialog.png']
3. Enter a new numbering symbol.
4. Click **OK**.

To remove the element’s number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element from which you want to remove the number and click the Remove button.
3. You can select either to remove the number only from the selected element or to remove numbers from all elements owned by the selected elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove button. Only the selected element’s number is removed.
  - Click the Remove Recursively button. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To increase the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to increase.
3. Click the Increase button. The element number is increased by one.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To decrease the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to decrease.
3. Click the **Decrease** button. The element number is decreased by one. The **Decrease** button is available, when the selected element’s number is not the first in the list.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To renumber the elements

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click Details to extent the dialog with the renumbering commands.
3. You can select either to renumber only elements listed in the element list or to renumber elements listed in the element list including their owning elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Renumber** button. All the elements in the elements list are renumbered successively.
  - Click the **Renumber Recursively** button. All the elements and elements owned by the selected element are renumbered successively.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

If the **Renumber from Initial Value** is selected, the whole element number changes. Otherwise just the number of the selected level changes.

To add a prefix to the element number

1. On the element owner’s shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Prefix column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the prefix. It can be any symbol.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates how numbers with prefixes and without prefixes are depicted on the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a separator in the element number

1. Open the element owner’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Separator column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the separator symbol. A new separator will override the earlier predefined separator.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates the number separator changing from “.” to “/”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a numbering scheme

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the appropriate Numbered property row, click the Numbering Scheme cell to open the list of the defined numbering schemes.
3. Select the desired numbering scheme in that list.
4. Click OK .

When you change the numbering scheme for the selected element, the new numbering scheme is applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

The following example illustrates how numbers are depicted on the element shape after changing the numbering scheme from numerical to literal.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the last ID

1. Right-click an element numbered in the sequence, whose last ID you want to update.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The appropriate dialog opens.
3. Click Details and then click the Update Last ID button.
4. Wait while the message informs you that the last ID is set to the particular value. This value is the highest ID in the sequence.
5. Click OK to close the Element Numbering dialog.

As a result, all subsequently created elements are numbered continuing from the the highest ID. This is especially useful after you delete one or more elements from the end of the numbered sequence. In such cases, the last ID remains higher than it is expected to be, causing a numbering gap in the sequence. Updating the last ID prevents the emergence of that gap.

However, if a numbering gap is not at the end of the sequence, it remains even after the update of the last ID.

For better understanding the functionality, let's study a few cases.

1. If you have a sequence from 1 to 5, and delete elements with ID = 4 and ID = 5, the ID of a subsequently created element by default is 6. But if you update the last ID in advance, the one of the subsequently created element is 4 as it should be.
2. If you have a sequence from 1 to 3 and from 5 to 6, the last ID after the update is 6. As you can see, ID = 4 is skipped, because it isn't the highest ID in the sequence; the highest ID is 6.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713395 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00805: Manually configuring properties file

- page_id: `136713395`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713395/Manually+configuring+properties+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:31.966+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Used Projects Auto Update Plugin
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains how to correctly configure a Used Projects Auto Update Plugin properties file.

To configure the properties file for updating used projects

- In the <modeling tool installation directory>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.twcusageupdater directory, open the sample.properties file (or any generated properties file) through a wizard.
- Specify the properties values listed below in the table (or leave the default values if they do not need to be changed).

| Properties | Description |
| --- | --- |
| twc.server | TWCloud server hostname or IP (with port if it is not default) |
| twc.user.name | TWCloud username |
| twc.user.password | TWCloud user hashed password (the only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.project | TWCloud main project URI* |
| twc.project.password | Project hashed password if there is password protection (the only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.used.projects | List of used projects URI (with hashed passwords if project is password protected. Pattern <URI>,<URI>#<password>,<URI>.The only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.use.ssl | Use SSL flag |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains how to correctly configure a Used Projects Auto Update Plugin <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="673df12e-e0fe-4a61-9a15-de980f2193be">properties</ac:inline-comment-marker> file. </p><p><br /></p><p>To configure the properties file for updating used projects</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.twcusageupdater</em> directory, open the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f14e4879-635b-4b52-8a9f-0082b669b5c5">sample.properties</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f14e4879-635b-4b52-8a9f-0082b669b5c5"> file (or any generated properties file) through a wizard.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Specify the properties values listed below in the table (or leave the default values if they do not need to be changed). </li></ul><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Properties</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><em>twc.server</em></td><td>TWCloud server hostname or IP (with port if it is not default)</td></tr><tr><td><em>twc.user.name</em></td><td>TWCloud username</td></tr><tr><td><em>twc.user.password</em></td><td>TWCloud user hashed <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a90fb439-006e-4312-8476-7c2fef0f8f1b">password (t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects Auto Update Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> first)</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td><em>twc.project</em></td><td>TWCloud main project URI*</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><em>twc.project.password</em></td><td colspan="1"><span>Project <span>hashed </span>password if there is password protection <span>(t</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects Auto Update Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> first)</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><em>twc.used.projects</em></td><td><p>List of used projects URI (with <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="64ea3a97-200d-48a9-ab8d-3be608cd4a12">hashed </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="36343f8c-1181-464d-a027-2f31515f7f0f"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="64ea3a97-200d-48a9-ab8d-3be608cd4a12">password</ac:inline-comment-marker>s</ac:inline-comment-marker> if project is password protected. Pattern &lt;URI&gt;,&lt;URI&gt;#&lt;password&gt;,&lt;URI&gt;.</p><p>T<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects Auto Update Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> first)</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><em>twc.use.ssl</em></td><td colspan="1">Use SSL flag</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709832 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00807: Mapping ports on a Part type change

- page_id: `136709832`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709832/Mapping+ports+on+a+Part+type+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:10.188+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] type change, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.

Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the **Port Mapping** dialog appears.

##### Rules of automatic port mapping

Ports are mapped automatically:

- When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:
  - Port type and name
  - Port type (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type)
  - Port name (valid if the type of the original Port is not specified)
- Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024x'] .
- When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).
- When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type.

##### The Port Mapping dialog

The **Port Mapping** dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the elements of the **Port Mapping** dialog described in the following table.

| Element number | Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 |  | Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 2 |  | Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 3 |  | Displays lines between the mapped ports. |
| 4 |  | Creates a new port in the To list. In the From list, click a port and then drag it to the "Drag port(s) here to create a new port" box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped. |

To map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the **To** box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto a port in the **To** box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port.

To create a new Port in the **To** box

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto the following box: "*Drag**port(s)**here**to**create**a**new**port*". The new Port is created in the **To** box and ports are mapped. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto the box: "*Drag port(s) here to create a new port*". The selected number of ports will be created.

To un-map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select the line between the mapped ports.
2. Press the Delete key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>On a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> type change, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.</p><p>Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog appears.</p><h3>Rules of automatic port mapping</h3><p>Ports are mapped automatically:</p><ul><li>When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:<ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port type and name</strong></li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">type</strong> (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type) </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port name</strong> (valid if the type of the original Port is <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">not</strong> specified) </li></ul></li><li>Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interface" /></ac:link>. </li><li>When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).</li><li>When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2018e296-0847-4d0a-8481-8d55c293ae7f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto a port in the <strong>To</strong> box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>To create a new Port in the <strong>To</strong> box</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port in the <strong>From</strong> box.</li><li><p>Drag the selected Port onto the following box: &quot;<em>Drag</em><em>port(s)</em><em>here</em><em>to</em><em>create</em><em>a</em><em>new</em><em>port</em>&quot;. The new Port is created in the <strong>To</strong> box and ports are mapped.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc09ca29-20ea-4daa-b80c-d08fa53fe280"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto the box: &quot;<em>Drag port(s) here to create a new port</em>&quot;. The selected number of ports will be created. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>To un-map ports in the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the line between the mapped ports.</li><li>Press the <strong>Delete</strong> key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped. </li></ol><p> </p><p style="text-align: center;" /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711874 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00809: Marking elements as auxiliary

- page_id: `136711874`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711874/Marking+elements+as+auxiliary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:00.437+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639285959

639285961

639285960

An auxiliary element is an element needed in the model but not necessarily visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']. Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.

To mark an element as auxiliary

1. Select the element in the Model Browser.
2. In the Properties tab, select the Tags tab.
3. Set the treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject tag value to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the tag value.

The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option **Show Auxiliary Resources**is selected in the Options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Options drop-down list.

You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']**of your tool.

To show auxiliary resources by default in your model

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the search box, type auxiliary . All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.
3. Set the Show Auxiliary Resources property value to true .

639285958

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.</p><p><br /></p><p>To mark an element as auxiliary</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the element in the Model Browser.</li><li>In the <strong>Properties </strong>tab, select the <strong>Tags</strong> tab.</li><li>Set the <strong>treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject</strong> tag  value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tag_auxiliary.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Marking elements as auxiliary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Setting the tag value.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources </strong>is selected in the Options.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_auxiliary_resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Marking elements as auxiliary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Options drop-down list.</h6><p>You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>of your tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show auxiliary resources by default in your model</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. </li><li>In the search box, type <em>auxiliary</em>. All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8ad7878-8e57-4933-8a55-70b379706605"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639285958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715264 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00810: MDEL hyperlink

- page_id: `136715264`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715264/MDEL+hyperlink
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:38:46.486+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model > Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.

For that purpose the **mdel://** protocol is introduced.

Use c*om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils* to retrieve mdel URL of elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.</p><p>For that purpose the <strong>mdel://</strong> protocol is introduced. </p><p>Use c<em>om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em> to retrieve mdel URL of elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715118 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00811: MDP protocol

- page_id: `136715118`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715118/MDP+protocol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:06.682+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Adding new functionality > Working with icons
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler **mdp://**for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project.

Usage samples:

- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.
- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.

**URL syntax**

| mdp://resource/ | Use the "resource" host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the activitydiagram.png icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png |
| --- | --- |
| mdp://attachedFile/ | Use the "attachedFile" host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID "_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288", use such URL:mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler <strong>mdp:// </strong>for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project. </p><div><p>Usage samples:</p><ul><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.</li><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.</li></ul><p> </p><p><strong>URL syntax</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td>mdp://resource/</td><td><p>Use the &quot;<em>resource</em>&quot; host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the <em>activitydiagram.png</em> icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:</p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png</span></pre></td></tr><tr><td>mdp://attachedFile/</td><td><p><span>Use the &quot;<em>attachedFile</em>&quot; host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID &quot;<em>_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</em>&quot;, use such URL:</span></p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</span></pre></td></tr></tbody></table></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709317 space=MD2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00812: Memory allocation

- page_id: `136709317`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709317/Memory+allocation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-25T13:48:01.380+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Improving performance
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Low memory notification

1427463063

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Low memory notification

1427463065

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Low memory notification

1427463064

INLINE

If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.

#### TIP: Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.

##### Recommended amount of allocated memory

The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.

##### Changing the amount of allocated memory

You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Memory Settings category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Maximum Heap Size option. You should only change the value of the Thread Stack Size option if a *java.lang.StackOverflowError*error occurs.

#### TIP: Low memory notification

Low memory notification

While working on the project, you can get the *Low memory* notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click *Open Environment Options*to open the **Environment options** dialog where you can adjust memory settings.

To enable/disable notifications about low memory

1. Go to Environment Options > Display > Notify about low memory option

- 
  - Set to true to show notifications about low memory.
  - Set to false to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t his notification can be disabled, by clicking Do not show again option in the Low memory notification.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the modeling tool properties file.
2. In the **JAVA_ARGS** line, change the value of the **-Xmx** property. For example, change the **-Xmx800M** to **-Xmx1066M**.
3. Save and close the file.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine

1. Go to Control Panel > System and Security > System > Advanced system settings. The System Properties dialog opens.
2. In the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables . The Environment Variables dialog opens.
3. In the System variables section, do one of the following:
  1. If the _JAVA_OPTIONS variable is not in the variables list, click New . New System Variable dialog opens.
  2. If the **_JAVA_OPTIONS** variable is in the variables list, select it and click **Edit**.**Edit System Variable**dialog opens.
4. Set **Variable name** to **_JAVA_OPTIONS** and **Variable value** to the desired memory amount, e.g. **-Xmx1066M**. Click **OK**. Changing options via the **Environment Variables** dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.

1427463062

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715808 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00813: Memory and Disk Space Requirements for Large Models

- page_id: `136715808`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715808/Memory+and+Disk+Space+Requirements+for+Large+Models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:46.337+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Guidelines for Working with Large Models
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Memory

The memory allocation for the modeling tool by project size:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |

The amount of required memory proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

1the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x (decrease by 40-80% in SysML and UAF projects).

2these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.

The operations that require more memory (1, 2, and 3.5 million of 2021x project elements respectively):

- UPDM migration to UAF - 10GB, 10GB, 36GB
- Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB
- Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB 3

3strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).

##### Disk Space

The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Disk Space |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 19.0 | 2021x |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 10GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 30GB |

The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

**Note:** Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.

Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

10GB, 10GB, 36GB</li><li>Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB</li><li>Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB <sup>3</sup></li></ul><p><sup>3 </sup>strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).</p><h3>Disk Space</h3><p>The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files: </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Project Elements Count<br />(including used projects)</th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">Disk Space</th></tr><tr><th style="text-align: center;">19.0</th><th style="text-align: center;">2021x</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">6GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;">2.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">2 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">10GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">3.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">30GB</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.</p><p><strong>Note:</strong> Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.</p><p>Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709311 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00814: Memory monitor

- page_id: `136709311`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709311/Memory+monitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:42.605+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Improving performance
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Running out of memory

1427409680

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Running out of memory

1427409682

238600894

**On this page:**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Running out of memory

1427409681

When working with larger models, turn on the Memory Monitor to monitor the memory used by a modeling tool. The Memory Monitor shows currently used memory and the current Java heap size. Typically, when you start working with a model, used memory increases because actions are stored in the undo list, and opened diagrams are loaded.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The memory monitor bar showing currently used memory and the current Java heap size.

##### Turning on the Memory Monitor

To turn on the Memory Monitor

- In the main menu of a modeling tool, select View > Status Line > Show Memory Monitor . The Memory Monitor bar appears in the bottom-right corner of the modeling tool window.

#### WARNING: Running out of memory

Running out of memory

When used memory takes more than 85% of the total heap size, the Memory Monitor bar turns red, indicating that a modeling tool may run out of memory soon.

##### Freeing unused memory with the Garbage Collector

If a modeling tool uses all the Java heap size, you can try to recover enough memory for saving a project with the Garbage Collector, but this may take some time.

To free unused memory with the Garbage Collector

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar and select Run Garbage Collector .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Memory Monitor pop-up window

If you find it impossible to use the modeling tool and/or see the Out of Memory error, you should dump the memory heap to the**.hprof**file that is created in the defined location on the computer and then submit this file to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC'] for further assistance regarding the memory consumption issue.

##### Dumping the memory heap

To dump the memory heap

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Heap , and then set the desired name and location of the memory heap file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory heap file.

You must create a new file for the heap dump since the memory heap cannot be dumped into an existing file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory heap

If the Memory Monitor bar is still red, save the model, restart the modeling tool, and reload the model. If the Memory Monitor bar stays red after reloading the model, you should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space=''].

##### Dumping the memory histogram

To dump the memory histogram

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Histogram , and then set the desired name and location of the memory histogram file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory histogram file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory histogram

1427409679

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
