Typically, when you start working with a model, used memory increases because actions are stored in the undo list, and opened diagrams are loaded.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="memory_monitoring.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The memory monitor bar showing currently used memory and the current Java heap size.</h6><h3>Turning on the Memory Monitor</h3><p>To turn on the Memory Monitor</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Status Line</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Memory Monitor</strong>. The Memory Monitor bar appears in the bottom-right corner of the modeling tool window.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d48978ce-5014-42b7-8c1d-3328ec4d0baf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Running out of memory</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When used memory takes more than 85% of the total heap size, the Memory Monitor bar turns red, indicating that a modeling tool may run out of memory soon.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Freeing unused memory with the Garbage Collector</span></h3><p>If a modeling tool uses all the Java heap size, you can try to recover enough memory for saving a project with the Garbage Collector, but this may take some time.</p><p><br />To free unused memory with the Garbage Collector</p><hr /><ul><li>Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar and select <strong>Run Garbage Collector</strong>. <br /><br /></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_garbage_collector.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Memory Monitor pop-up window</h6><p><br /></p><p>If you find it impossible to use the modeling tool and/or see the Out of Memory error, you should dump the memory heap to the<strong> .hprof </strong>file that is created in the defined location on the computer and then submit this file to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for further assistance regarding the memory consumption issue. </p><h3>Dumping the memory heap</h3><p>To dump the memory heap</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select <strong>Dump Memory Heap</strong>, and then set the desired name and location of the memory heap file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp. </li><li>Press Ctrl+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory heap file. </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="514b3a97-9dbd-4b7c-94ef-e8f13e73bb5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must create a new file for the heap dump since the memory heap cannot be dumped into an existing file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dump_memory_heap.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dumping memory heap</h6><p>If the Memory Monitor bar is still red, save the model, restart the modeling tool, and reload the model. If the Memory Monitor bar stays red after reloading the model, you should <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[increase the maximum Java heap size]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3>Dumping the memory histogram</h3><p>To dump the memory histogram</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select <strong>Dump Memory Histogram</strong>, and then set the desired name and location of the memory histogram file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory histogram file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03cb561f-6337-499d-8d23-5cac3e0ded5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the <strong>Dump Memory Histogram</strong> functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dump_memory_histogram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dumping memory histogram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="628e74f3-7e58-465a-bdb6-53f96626f15a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427409679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Background Task Manager" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715313 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00815: Merging and differencing

- page_id: `136715313`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715313/Merging+and+differencing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:22.186+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:

- Merge - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.
- Compare - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip once you have the modeling tool installed.

Project merging consists of three steps:

1. Discovering project changes (differences) . The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.
2. Accepting / rejecting changes . All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.
3. Applying accepted changes . Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).

There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.

The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****You can find the code examples:

- for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\merge
- for Compare API: < installation_direc tory>\openapi\examples\compare

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:</p><ul><li><strong>Merge</strong> - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip</em> once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.</li><li><strong>Compare</strong> - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip</em> once you have the modeling tool installed.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Project merging consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><strong>Discovering project changes (differences)</strong>. The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.</li><li><strong>Accepting / rejecting changes</strong>. All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.</li><li><strong>Applying accepted changes</strong>. Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).</li></ol><p>There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.</p><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MergeDomain.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Merging and differencing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples:</p><ul><li>for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\merge</em></li><li>for Compare API: &lt;<em>installation_direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\compare</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715001 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00816: Merging property groups and subgroups

- page_id: `136715001`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715001/Merging+property+groups+and+subgroups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:00.182+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Creating Property Groups and Subgroups
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify property groups and subgroups for different customization elements of the same element. That element will have groups and subgroups from all customization elements.

#### INFO: Example

Example

Let’s say we have two customizations of the same element type, the actor. The first customization has the property group *My Group* with the subgroup *Subgroup 1* containing the property *Prop 1*. The second customization has the same property group with the same subgroup specified, but the subgroup *Subgroup 1* contains the property *Prop 2* .

As a result, the actor element type will have *My Group,* with *Subgroup 1* containing both*Prop 1* and *Prop 2*.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Property Groups and Subgroups' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>You can specify property groups and subgroups for different customization elements of the same element. That element will have groups and subgroups from all customization elements. </span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d70bdea3-8b22-4fee-9042-256bf3a1707e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Let’s say we have two customizations of the same element type, the actor. The first customization has the property group </span> <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="63646f21-6f23-4d63-a774-0eed08769f53">My Group</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em> <span>with the subgroup </span> <span><em>Subgroup 1</em> </span> <span>containing the property </span> <em>Prop 1 </em><span>. The second customization has the same property group with the same subgroup specified, but the subgroup </span> <em>Subgroup 1 </em> <span>contains the property </span> <em>Prop 2</em> <span>. </span></p><p>As a result, the actor element type will have <em>My Group,</em> with <em>Subgroup 1</em> containing both<em> Prop 1</em> and <em>Prop 2</em>. </p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> <br /> </span></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e78ddfd-bec9-4837-bd51-242520587f8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Property Groups and Subgroups" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711124 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00817: Message

- page_id: `136711124`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711124/Message
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:52:10.549+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['synchcall', 'asynchcall', 'asynchsignal', 'reply', 'createmessage', 'deletemessage', 'message-syntax', 'message-sort']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

372179598

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

372179600

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

372179599

#### EXCERPT: Example

INLINE

A Message defines a particular communication between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space=''] of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''], such as raising a Signal, invoking an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''], or creating or destroying an Instance. Messages specify the kind of communication, the sender, and the receiver.

A Message is represented by arrows between the lifelines. The style of the Message line and arrowhead reflect the types of the Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can see the description of a selected property in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. To see descriptions, be sure the **Show Description** option is selected.

##### Message sorts

| Message Sort | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| synchCall | The Message was generated by a synchronous call to an Operation. Synchronous Messages can overtake each other. When a Message represents an Operation, the arguments of the Message must correspond to the parameters of the Operation. |  |
| asynchCall | Asynchronous means that the caller continues immediately after the call. asynchCall Messages do not have reply Messages. |  |
| asynchSignal | The Message was generated by an asynchronous send Action. The argument of the Message must correspond to the attributes of the Signal. |  |
| reply | A reply Message to an Operation call. |  |
| createMessage | The Message designates the creation of another Lifeline object. No other Messages on a given Lifeline in an Interaction operand may appear above a Lifeline. |  |
| deleteMessage | The Message designates the termination of another Lifeline. |  |

##### Message syntax

The Message name is displayed on the message path on the diagram pane. The syntax of the message name is as follows:

*<messageident> ::= [<attribute> '='] <signal or operation name> ['(' [<argument> [','*

*<argument>]* ')'] [':' <return value>]*

*<argument> ::= ([<parameter name> '='] <argument-value>) / '-'*

#### TIP: Example

Example

*v=fo(P1="a", P2=True):12*

****

**Common actions with Messages**

To set an action type for a Message

- In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , click the Message Sort property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.
- On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.

To show / hide Message numbers

1. Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.
2. Then do one of the following:

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] or Time diagram , select / clear Show Message Numbers .
- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space=''] , select / clear Numbering > Show Message Numbers .

372179596

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="822ba8cc-c0ec-4d97-8f11-8258e93001ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179598</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b139e57a-2206-4ce5-aee2-a45e8fa1106e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179600</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b5c4104-9220-4501-bdb9-b25940ec62a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179599</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e751a1-0bf7-4bf6-8901-22b44fe37360"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Message defines a particular communication between <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, such as raising a Signal, invoking an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link>, or creating or destroying an Instance. Messages specify the kind of communication, the sender, and the receiver.</p><p>A Message is represented by arrows between the lifelines. The style of the Message line and arrowhead reflect the types of the Message.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Example of Message in Sequence diagram" ac:alt="Example of Message in Sequence diagram" ac:height="192" ac:width="292"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_message_sequence_diagram.png" /></ac:image>    <ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Example of Message in Communication diagram" ac:alt="Example of Message in Communication diagram" ac:height="230" ac:width="367"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_message_communication_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br />You can see the description of a selected property in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. To see descriptions, be sure the <strong>Show Description</strong> option is selected.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Message sorts</span></h3><div class="WordSection3"><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Message Sort</p></th><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">synchCall</td><td colspan="1"><p>The Message was generated by a synchronous call to an Operation. Synchronous Messages can overtake each other. When a Message represents an Operation, the arguments of the Message must correspond to the parameters of the Operation.</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="105" ac:width="230"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchCall_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">asynchCall</td><td colspan="1">Asynchronous means that the caller continues immediately after the call. asynchCall Messages do not have reply Messages.</td><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="109" ac:width="291"><ri:attachment ri:filename="asynchCall_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">asynchSignal</td><td colspan="1">The Message was generated by an asynchronous send Action. The argument of the Message must correspond to the attributes of the Signal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">reply</td><td colspan="1">A reply Message to an Operation call.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="127" ac:width="226"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reply_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">createMessage</td><td colspan="1">The Message designates the creation of another Lifeline object. No other Messages on a given Lifeline in an Interaction operand may appear above a Lifeline.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="149" ac:width="281"><ri:attachment ri:filename="createMessage.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">deleteMessage</td><td colspan="1">The Message designates the termination of another Lifeline.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="135" ac:width="201"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deleteMessage.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Message syntax</h3><p>The Message name is displayed on the message path on the diagram pane. The syntax of the message name is as follows:</p><p><em>&lt;messageident&gt; ::= [&lt;attribute&gt; '='] &lt;signal or operation name&gt; ['(' [&lt;argument&gt; [','</em></p><p><em>&lt;argument&gt;]* ')'] [':' &lt;return value&gt;]</em></p><p><em>&lt;argument&gt; ::= ([&lt;parameter name&gt; '='] &lt;argument-value&gt;) / '-'</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32eca1a1-94ec-4a40-9b52-7326980fbe2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <em>v=fo(P1=&quot;a&quot;, P2=True):12</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;">Common actions with Messages</strong></p><p>To set an action type for a Message</p><hr />Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Message Sort</strong> property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show / hide Message numbers</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.</li><li>Then do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or <span class="confluence-link">Time diagram</span>, select / clear <strong>Show Message Numbers</strong>.</li><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /></ac:link>, select / clear <strong>Numbering</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Message Numbers</strong>.</li></ul></div></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00553dca-886e-43a4-8b6a-db1a96fe3ade"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179596</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7aad2a92-6694-4cce-a033-59f3b3d47542" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711140 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00818: Message name parsing

- page_id: `136711140`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711140/Message+name+parsing
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:24:38.765+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['parsing-rules']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372310483

372310488

372310487

INLINE

Arguments, parameters, and return values specified on a Message in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] can be parsed and new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']can be created. Parsing and mapping Message names to real model elements (operations, parameters, etc.) allows for easy transition from prototype Sequence diagrams to complete Sequence diagrams representing actual Interactions between system parts.

##### Message name parsing

On a Message name area on a diagram pane, you can type the name of a new Operation together with arguments, parameters, and return value. After you click on the button near the Message name, the new Operation will be created, together with its specifications.

##### Message syntax

The whole Message name should be written according to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''].

##### Message Parsing notification message

If any Message name syntax errors are found, users are warned and given suggestions for fixing these mistakes. The Message Parsing notification message opens, showing the .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the preceding example, you can see that the following appears on the Message name:

*getUserAddress(city=”Allen”, street=”700 Central Expy S”, No=”110”)*. After you click the Create New Operation (this message button [IMAGE alt='' src='']), the new *getUserAddress* Operation will be created. The following Message arguments will be created - *Allen*, *700 Central Expy S*, and *110*, together with the corresponding parameters - *city*, *street*, *No*.

To create a new operation from the diagram pane

1. Create a call Message (asynchCall or synchCall) between two Lifelines. The Lifeline that the call Message is connected to must have a type specified.The preceding figure shows that the *Address* type is assigned to the second Lifeline.
2. On the diagram pane, on the message, type the message name according to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''].
3. After you have typed the Message name, exit the edit mode by clicking on the diagram pane.
4. Select the Message label again. The Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] is displayed next to the Message name.
5. Click the Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . A new Operation is created and represented in the message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , the **Signature (operation)** property cell . Note that a new Operation is created only after you click the Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. If you do not click this button, the Message label will be treated as a Message name.If the syntax is written incorrectly, the opens.

##### Parsing rules

The following table describes Message name samples and parsing results:

| Purpose | Message name typed on the diagram pane | Action after typing on the message label | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create a new Operation | login () | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login ()The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created. |
| After click on the Create New Operation according to this message button , a new Operation is always created. A new Operation is created even if an Operation with thesame name already exists. |  |  |  |
| Create a new Operation with an argument | login (“John”) | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login (unnamed1=”John”)The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the message Specification window > the Arguments property group, the following argument is created: John. For the Lifeline type, the login Operation is created.The login Operation’s parameter is created and with the message argument. |
| Different kinds of arguments are supported. For the following arguments the following value specifications are created:John - Opaque Expression12345 - Literal IntegerTrue - Literal Boolean“John” - Literal String (always when quotation-marks are used).For more information about value specification, see . |  |  |  |
| Create a new Operation with a parameter and an argument | login (username=”John”) | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login (username=”John”) The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Arguments property group, the following argument is created: John. For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created. The login operation’s parameter is created and with the Message argument. |
| Create a new Operation with a return parameter | login () : true | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login () : trueThe Operation is listed in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Arguments property group the following argument is created: true.For the classifier assigned to the Lifeline, the login Operation is created, together with the parameter.The login Operation’s parameter with the return direction is created. The Operation’s parameter is with the Message argument. |
| Create a new Operation for the Message together with a reply Message | status = login () | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: status = login ()The Operation is listed in the message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Reply message property cell, the replay Message is created. (The Replay message property cell is displayed in Expert mode only.)3. The status argument is created in the replay Message. |
| Create a new argument for the MessageIf this is valid, the argument is added straight from the M essage label for the already- existing O peration. | Before was:login (username=John)Add theargument 1282:login (username=John, 1284) | Exit the Message label edit mode by clicking thediagram pane. | A new argument is created and the Message is highlighted with yellow color; that is, the validation warning appears informing you that a parameter is missing and the Message argument is not synchronized with the operation parameter.To resolve the validation warning:Click the Message. The Message smart manipulator toolbar displays.Click the button to synchronize the Message argument with the Operation parameter. |
| Update the argument | Before was:login (John)Now change the argument to:login (Joseph) | Exit the message label edit mode by clicking the diagram pane. | The argument is updated. |

##### incorrect_synaxNotification message about incorrect syntax

If the Message syntax is incorrect, you will receive the following notification message at the bottom right corner of the MagicDraw window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the first line of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see an incorrect message name. In the example, the incorrect Message name is *getUser Address(city=)*. Due to the incorrect Message name, parsing cannot be executed and a new Operation cannot be created. In the second paragraph of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see the explanation of which syntax is expected. Next, you can see an example of the correct Message syntax.

372310480

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a417965-db89-4c29-87ff-4d77d1a60dfe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372310483</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21556b2d-623a-459b-bbb0-3ada9014691a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372310488</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5062409c-ec11-4f12-a83a-5dc80399da99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372310487</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e751a1-0bf7-4bf6-8901-22b44fe37360"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Arguments, parameters, and return values specified on a Message in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link> can be parsed and new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>can be created. Parsing and mapping Message names to real model elements (operations, parameters, etc.) allows for easy transition from prototype Sequence diagrams to complete Sequence diagrams representing actual Interactions between system parts.</p><h3>Message name parsing</h3><p>On a Message name area on a diagram pane, you can type the name of a new Operation together with arguments, parameters, and return value. After you click on the button near the Message name, the new Operation will be created, together with its specifications.</p><h3>Message syntax</h3><p>The whole Message name should be written according to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Message syntax rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Message Parsing notification message</h3><p>If any Message name syntax errors are found, users are warned and given suggestions for fixing these mistakes. The Message Parsing notification message opens, showing the <ac:link ac:anchor="incorrect_syntax"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[concrete error]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of Message parsing" ac:alt="Example of Message parsing"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_parsing.png" /></ac:image> </p><p>In the preceding example, you can see that the following appears on the Message name: </p><p><em>getUserAddress(city=”Allen”, street=”700 Central Expy S”, No=”110”)</em>. After you click the Create New Operation (this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>), the new <em>getUserAddress</em> Operation will be created. The following Message arguments will be created - <em>Allen</em>, <em>700 Central Expy S</em>, and <em>110</em>, together with the corresponding parameters - <em>city</em>, <em>street</em>, <em>No</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new operation from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create a call Message (asynchCall or synchCall) between two Lifelines.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cb4d87e-afcf-485b-90db-b317eca475b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Lifeline that the call Message is connected to must have a type specified.The preceding figure shows that the <em>Address</em> type is assigned to the second Lifeline.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>On the diagram pane, on the message, type the message name according to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[syntax rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>After you have typed the Message name, exit the edit mode by clicking on the diagram pane.</p></li><li><p>Select the Message label again. A new Operation is created and represented in the message </span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">, the </span> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ba1e0a45-269c-47fe-aa3d-ed018ec1ee4b">Signature (operation)</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ba1e0a45-269c-47fe-aa3d-ed018ec1ee4b"> property cell</ac:inline-comment-marker> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7864bef3-c13d-4a7a-a213-9302518f2f79"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that a new Operation is created only after you click the Create New Operation according to this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. If you do not click this button, the Message label will be treated as a Message name.</p><p>If the syntax is written incorrectly, the <ac:link ac:anchor="incorrect_synta"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Message Parsing window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> opens.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Parsing rules</h3><p><br />The following table describes Message name samples and parsing results:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Purpose</th><th><p>Message name typed on the diagram pane</p></th><th><p>Action after typing on the message label</p></th><th>Result</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>Create a new Operation</p></td><td><em>login ()</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the Create New Operation according to this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">login ()</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is referenced in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b252c9c-01ab-4c37-b6c9-085eed94e15b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After click on the Create New Operation according to this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, a new Operation is always created. For the following arguments the following value specifications are created:</p><p><em>John</em> - Opaque Expression</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">12345</em> - Literal Integer</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">True</em> - Literal Boolean</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">“John”</em> - Literal String (always when quotation-marks are used).</p><p>For more information about value specification, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning value specification as property value" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Create a new O<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">peration with </span>a parameter and an argument</td><td><p><em>login (username=”John”)</em></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper">Click the Create <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">New Operation </span> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">according to this </span> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p> The new operation is created: <em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">login (username=”John”)</span> </em></p><ol><li>The Operation is referenced in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Arguments</strong> property group, the following argument is created: <em>John</em>. </li><li>For the Lifeline type, the <em>login</em> operation is created. </li><li>The <em>login</em> operation’s parameter is created and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[synchronized]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the Message argument.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create a new Operation with a return parameter</p></td><td><em>login () : true</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span>Click the Create </span> <span>New Operation </span> <span>according to this </span> <span>message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em>login () : true</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is listed in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Arguments</strong> property group the following argument is created: <em>true</em>.</li><li>For the classifier assigned to the Lifeline, the <em>login</em> Operation is created, together with the parameter.</li><li>The <em>login</em> Operation’s parameter with the return direction is created. The Operation’s parameter is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[synchronized]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the Message argument.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create a new Operation for the Message together with a reply Message</p></td><td><em>status = login ()</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span>Click the Create </span> <span>New Operation </span> <span>according to this </span> <span>message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em>status = login ()</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is listed in the message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Reply message</strong> property cell, the replay Message is created. (The <strong>Replay message</strong> property cell is displayed in Expert mode only.)</li></ol><p>3. The <em>status</em> argument is created in the replay Message.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Create a new argument for the Message</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc2828c6-d551-4405-b485-f7f01c4e8142"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If this is </span> <span>valid, the </span> <span>argument is </span> <span>added straight </span> <span>from the M</span> <span>essage label </span> <span>for the already-</span> <span>existing O</span> <span>peration.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><p>Before was:</p><p><em>login (username=John)</em></p><p>Add the<br />argument <em>1282</em>:</p><p><em>login (username=John, 1284)</em></p></td><td><p>Exit the Message label edit mode by clicking the<br />diagram pane.</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A new argument is created and the Message is highlighted with yellow color; that is, the validation warning appears informing you that a parameter is missing and the Message argument is not synchronized with the operation parameter.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_parsing3.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To resolve the validation warning:</p><ol><li>Click the Message. The Message smart manipulator toolbar displays.</li><li><p>Click the button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_invalid.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to synchronize the Message argument with the Operation parameter.</p></li></ol></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update the argument</p></td><td><p>Before was:</p><p><em>login (John)</em></p><p>Now change the argument to:</p><p><em>login (Joseph)</em></p></td><td><p>Exit the message label edit mode by clicking the diagram pane.</p></td><td>The argument is updated.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c29e24d-add7-4b14-9860-87ebefc4f27d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">incorrect_synax</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Notification message about incorrect syntax</h3><p><br />If the Message syntax is incorrect, you will receive the following notification message at the bottom right corner of the MagicDraw window.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of Message Parsing notification message" ac:alt="Example of Message Parsing notification message"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_parsing2.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the first line of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see an incorrect message name. In the example, the incorrect Message name is <em>getUser Address(city=)</em>. Due to the incorrect Message name, parsing cannot be executed and a new Operation cannot be created. In the second paragraph of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see the explanation of which syntax is expected. Next, you can see an example of the correct Message syntax.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d91674ef-cf72-4b83-98fd-96a77a5e21fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372310480</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711172 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00819: Message Occurrence Specification

- page_id: `136711172`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711172/Message+Occurrence+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:26.340+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Message Occurrence Specification specifies the occurrence of message events, such as sending and receiving signals or invoking or receiving operation calls. A Message Occurrence Specification is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages' space='MD2024x'] end. Messages are generated either by synchronous operation calls or asynchronous signal sends. They are received by the execution of the corresponding accept event actions. 
In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], you can modify an element and add various specifications to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Message Occurrence Specification specifies the occurrence of message events, such as sending and receiving signals or invoking or receiving operation calls. A Message Occurrence Specification is a kind of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Messages" /></ac:link> end. Messages are generated either by synchronous operation calls or asynchronous signal sends. They are received by the execution of the corresponding accept event actions. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the M<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">essage <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify an element and add various specifications to it.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711160 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00820: Messages in Communication diagrams

- page_id: `136711160`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711160/Messages+in+Communication+diagrams
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:21:37.545+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['advanced-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372507889

372507891

372507890

INLINE

Messages in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space=''] can only be depicted on connectors; thus, you should draw a connector before assigning a Message to it.

The following steps show you how to work with messages:

##### Assigning a message to a connector

assignTo assign a message to a connector

1. On the diagram palette, click the button corresponding to the desired Message type.
2. Click a desired connector on the diagram pane. A Message arrow will be placed on the selected connector. A Message flow has two directions: right and left. Choose one of them by clicking the associated button on the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Setting/removing the advanced numbering of messages

set_removeTo set/remove the advanced numbering of messages

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Numbering .
2. Select to set, or clear to remove the Use Advanced Numbering .

##### Changing current message numbering

To change current message numbering

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Numbering > Change Numbering .
2. Increase, decrease, and / or change the level of numbering in the Change Communication Numbering dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_communication_numbering.png']

If the **Edit** button is inactive, of Messages.

##### Displaying messages

If you want to display messages on connectors in the Communication diagram you must first implement the following conditions (see the figure below):

- Messages are assigned to connectors . ]]>
- The lifeline shapes are already displayed on the diagram pane. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']]]>
- The paths between lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='']]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display messages

1. Select the connector between lifelines.
2. Perform one of the following actions: [ATTACHMENT filename='display_messages_command.png']
  - Right-click the connector to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Messages .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select Display Messages .
3. In the opened Select Messages dialog, select the messages you want to display, and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_messages_dialog.png'] Messages of the selected connector are displayed on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='displayed_messages.png']

372507887

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Communication diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='communication diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip.

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a04a962-fd24-4c37-9b54-55c969c1cb58"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372507889</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71fb2084-9484-466a-aeda-d4c28c07d6ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372507891</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21afd676-fd15-4c34-ba69-f49c850d0af5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372507890</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e751a1-0bf7-4bf6-8901-22b44fe37360"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Messages in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Communication diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can only be depicted on connectors; thus, you should draw a connector before assigning a Message to it.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following steps show you how to work with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">messages</span>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ee3d275-4e56-4906-821d-91ab82ced207" /></p><h3>Assigning a message to a connector</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59c40332-6026-4bbe-b9b0-53473acc5142"><ac:parameter ac:name="">assign</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To assign a message to a connector</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram palette, click the button corresponding to the desired Message type.</li><li><p> Click a desired connector on the diagram pane. A Message arrow will be placed on the <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">selected connector.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35c55837-efd9-490a-bf71-303d65e1e5af"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Message flow has two directions: right and left. Choose one of them by clicking the associated button on the diagram palette.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_flow_directions.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Setting/removing the advanced numbering of messages</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e8298c0-d650-4b55-8159-865631027944"><ac:parameter ac:name="">set_remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set/remove the advanced numbering of messages</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Numbering</strong>.</li><li>Select to set, or clear to remove the<strong> Use Advanced Numbering</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Changing current message numbering</h3><p>To change current message numbering</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Numbering</strong> &gt; <strong>Change Numbering</strong>.</li><li>Increase, decrease, and / or change the level of numbering in the <strong>Change Communication Numbering</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Change Communication Numbering dialog" ac:alt="Change Communication Numbering dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_communication_numbering.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89256661-9a14-4b76-9a7e-485ace915bbb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Edit</strong> button is inactive,<ac:link ac:anchor="set_remove"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ remove the automatic advanced numbering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of Messages.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying messages</h3><p>If you want to display messages on connectors in the Communication diagram you must first implement the following conditions (see the figure below):</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">Messages are assigned to connectors</span>. <ac:link ac:anchor="assign"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to assign a message to connector >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The lifeline shapes are already displayed on the diagram pane. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display lifelines >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The paths between lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display paths >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="The Loan Library Item Communication diagram which contains two lifelines and the connector between them." ac:alt="The Loan Library Item Communication diagram which contains two lifelines and the connector between them."><ri:attachment ri:filename="library_system_communication_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To display messages</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the connector between lifelines.</li><li>Perform one of the following actions:<br /><ul><li>Right-click the connector to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Messages</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"> <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>,</span>and select <strong>Display Messages</strong>.</li></ul><ac:image ac:title="The Display Message command." ac:alt="The Display Message command."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_messages_command.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the opened<strong> Select Messages</strong> dialog, select the messages you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Select Messages dialog." ac:alt="The Select Messages dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_messages_dialog.png" /></ac:image> <br />Messages of the selected connector are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Messages of the selected connector are displayed on the diagram pane." ac:alt="Messages of the selected connector are displayed on the diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayed_messages.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711150 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00821: Messages in Sequence and Time Diagrams

- page_id: `136711150`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711150/Messages+in+Sequence+and+Time+Diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:16:52.240+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['nested-activation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372457582

372457584

1277748670

**On this page:**

372457583

INLINE

Messages allow displaying an interaction between objects. A Message is labeled with either the message name or the assigned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''](signal) name and its arguments.

##### Drawing Message from/to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']to/from diagram frame

Outgoing and incoming messages communicate with the outside environment by connecting to the border gates (the diagram frame).

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the example above, you can see the Message *card inserted* that is drawn from the diagram frame border to the Lifeline *controller*. The Message *authorize* is created from the Lifeline *controller* to the diagram frame.

For a Message to be correct, its receive end should be known. However, we never know who will connect to the gate from the outside. Thus, we are using the reply Message when creating the Message from the Lifeline to the diagram frame (see Message *authorize* in the figure).

##### Creating nested activation

Nested activations allow you to model:

- A parallel execution of operations that belong to a single [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] .
- Callback messages.

The nested activation can be created between at least two messages that point to the same activation.

Nested activations can be created for the following messages:

1. Synchronous Call Message (synchCall)
2. Asynchronous Call Message (asynchCall)
3. Asynchronous Signal Message (asynchSignal)

createTo create a nested activation

Ensure that you have at least two messages pointing to the same activation in your model.

1. Select any subsequent Message.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation .
  - On the Message smart manipulator toolbar, click the Create Nested Activation button.

The Message will be connected to the nested activation.

To merge a nested activation with a parent activation

1. Select a Message that has a nested activation.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select Reduce Nesting Level .
  - On the Message smart manipulator toolbar, click the Reduce Nested Activation button.

The Message will be connected to the parent activation.

Nested activations can be used in the following cases:

- 
- 
- 

##### Modeling parallel executions

To model parallel executions for a non-active Lifeline

A non-active Lifeline is one that has a non-active Class as a type assigned. The non-active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to false. This property is available in the Expert mode.

The figure below shows the creation of a simple nested activation, described in the procedure .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

active_lifelineTo model parallel executions for an active Lifeline

An active Lifeline is one that has an active Class as a type assigned. The active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to true. This property is available in the Expert mode.

1. Select a Lifeline and from its shortcut menu, select Show Entire Activation . All activations of the selected Lifeline become all-in-one.
2. Create an outgoing Message or select an existing Message pointed to an active object. Be sure this is not the first one for the outgoing activation.
3. From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation . [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_activations_3.png']

##### Modeling callback Messages

callback_messageTo model a callback Message

1. Create or select an incoming Message that is modeled as a callback Message.
2. From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation . [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_activations_4.png']

##### Moving Messages

**Moving an individual Message**

You can move an individual Message up or down the lifeline simply by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message will be connected to a new execution specification.

To move an individual Message

1. Select the Message and drag it up or down the execution specification to reposition it. [ATTACHMENT filename='move_individual_message_1.png']
2. (Optional) Drag the Message above or below its execution specification to break the Message off its execution specification and connect it to a new one. [ATTACHMENT filename='move_individual_message_2.png']

**Moving a Message together with subsequent Messages**

If you want to move a Message up or down along with all of the subsequent Messages, as well as break the existing execution specification and connect the Messages to the new execution specifications, you can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pusher and magnet' space=''].

**Moving dependent Messages**

You can also move a Message together with its dependent Messages up or down the lifeline by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message, along with its dependent Messages, will be connected to the new execution specifications.

To move dependent Messages

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_dependent_messages_button.png'] Move Dependent Messages button.
2. On the diagram pane, select the Message you want to move. Dependent Messages are then selected automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Drag the selected Messages up or down the execution specification to reposition them.
4. (Optional) Drag the Messages above or below their execution specification to break the Messages off their execution specification and connect them to the new ones. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a Message is moved below or above its execution specification to break the latter and connect the Message to a new execution specification, the Message no longer depends on a Message it was dependent on prior to the breaking of the execution specification.

372457581

the selected Messages up or down the execution specification to reposition them.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Optional) Drag the Messages above or below their execution specification to break the Messages off their execution specification and connect them to the new ones. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_dependent_messages_2.png" /></ac:image> </span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1192f9c9-0174-4794-9d72-aae559299cb8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a Message is moved below or above its execution specification to break the latter and connect the Message to a new execution specification, the Message no longer depends on a Message it was dependent on prior to the breaking of the execution specification.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> <br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19082c2e-bcb5-4eab-b708-e2c4c13238a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372457581</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714951 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00822: Meta property substitution (changing name of UML property)

- page_id: `136714951`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714951/Meta+property+substitution+changing+name+of+UML+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:58.335+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Customizing Specification Window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize a standard UML property to represent a more restricted type. You can also rename the property.

###### Property name substitution

The following case study presents an example of property name substitution.

**Case study**

In the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], rename the Name property to *blockName*.

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024x']Create a *name* attribute in the customization element.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x']Apply the «*metaProperty*» stereotype on the attribute.
3. In the «*metaProperty*» attribute Specification window, click the **New Name** property, and then type a new name *blockName*.
4. To apply the changes, reopen the project.

###### Example of property name substitution.

###### Property description substitution

In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], the description of the selected property is displayed below the properties list (see the preceding figure). You can customize (change) the property description. For example, after changing the property name (see the Case Study above), you could also change the property description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of property description area in element Specification window .

****

**Case study**

****

To change the *Name* property description

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024x'] in the customization element.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x']Apply the «*metaProperty*» stereotype on the attribute.
3. Type a new description in the customization element Specification window, under the description attribute, **Documentation/Hyperlinks** property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='property_description2.png']
4. To apply the changes, save and reopen the project. The description of the *Name* property changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of property description substitution.

###### Type substitution (restriction)

To change the type

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024x']Create the *type* attribute for the customization element.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x']Apply the «*metaProperty*» stereotype.
3. Click the **New Types** property in the type *attribute* Specification window.
4. Specify the «Block» stereotype.
5. To apply the changes, save and reopen the project.

###### Suggesting predefined values

To create properties to list predefined values suggested for the end user (such as "type modifier")

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024x'] for the customization element.
2. Name attribute as a property to customize.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x'].
4. Enter suggested values into the Suggested Values property.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711173 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00823: Metaclass

- page_id: `136711173`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711173/Metaclass
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:26.398+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The abstract syntax of UML is specified using a UML model called the UML metamodel. Classes in a metamodel are called metaclasses. The detailed descriptions of each metaclass are provided in the [ATTACHMENT filename='UML specification.pdf'].

The metaclass icon in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] is [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Its shape is displayed with <metaclass> stereotype.

All metaclasses in our modeling tools are stored in the **Auxiliary Resources**, expand the **UML Standard Profile** Package > **UML 2 Metamodel** Package.

INLINE

To show the auxiliary resources

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable the Show Auxiliary Resources option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>The abstract syntax of UML is specified using a UML model called the UML metamodel. Classes in a metamodel are called metaclasses. The detailed descriptions of each metaclass are provided in the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="UML specification.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Metaclass" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</p><p>The metaclass icon in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> is <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metaclass_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Metaclass" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. Its shape is displayed with &lt;metaclass&gt; stereotype.</p><p>All metaclasses in our modeling tools are stored in the <strong>Auxiliary Resources</strong>, expand the <strong>UML Standard Profile</strong> Package &gt; <strong>UML 2 Metamodel</strong> Package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b3403b2-dbc7-4bb2-a5d3-ee8762888f81"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To show the auxiliary resources</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Metaclass" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and enable the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00824: Metamodel Reference

- page_id: `136712812`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712812/Metamodel+Reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:55.871+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ProfMeta

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a Profile has a metamodel reference.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Profile

**Solvers**

- To fix this, recreate the Profile to make sure it has a metamodel reference.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Profile *Prototype* does not have a metamodel reference. The error is resolved by recreating the Profile.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ProfMeta</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if a Profile has a metamodel reference.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Profile</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="571b51b2-3012-4df4-991a-e44744e60ccb">To fix this, recreate the Profile to make sure it has a metamodel reference.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Metamodel_Reference.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Metamodel Reference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Profile <em>Prototype</em> does not have a metamodel reference. The error is resolved by recreating the Profile.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715419 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00825: Method Implementation Reverse

- page_id: `136715419`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715419/Method+Implementation+Reverse
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:29:10.793+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

432174371

432174373

432174372

Java reverse to Sequence diagram functionality allows visualizing Java method implementation with UML Sequence diagram. Created from method Sequence diagram cannot be updated, every time new diagram should be generated.

To launch **Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard** and specify options needed for the reverse

- You are able to reverse any operation from the Browser: right click an operation, choose Reverse Implementation and launch Sequence diagram from Java Source Wizard .
- From the Tools menu, choose Model VIsualizer , and then choose Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard .
- When reversing, in the Reverse Options dialog box, choose Launch Model Visualizer and then choose Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard .

The more detailed example of how this functionality works, see MagicDraw Tutorials.pdf, which is locate in <MagicDraw installation directory>, manual folder.

432174370

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java Code Engineering' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard' space='']
